attributes.content:
## Overview

The Deployment card gives the workspace a structured home for deployment record: environment, version, timestamp, status, rollback reference.

As a first-class Boxel card, it can be linked to adjacent records, surfaced in filtered views, and reused in automation without the information collapsing into disconnected notes or one-off documents.

## Core Mechanics

Deployment is designed to participate in active workflows. Its state, relationships, and outputs need to remain inspectable so that agents and humans can collaborate on the same object without losing context.

The card can be created directly by a user, produced from a [[Workflow Playbook]], or updated in response to a [[Trigger]]. When AI is involved, a [[Prompt Template]] or [[Skill Card]] can shape how it is drafted, summarized, classified, or routed.

## Integration Points

- **[[Integration]]** -- Deployment can sync with or depend on external systems through the shared integration layer.
- **[[Trigger]]** -- Changes to Deployment can fire automation, and automation can also create or update Deployment.
- **[[Task]]** -- Deployment often creates follow-up work or is itself tracked through task execution.
- **[[Project]]** -- Deployment can be grouped under a larger initiative so its work stays visible in context.
- **[[Workflow Playbook]]** -- Deployment can be created, reviewed, or progressed as one step inside a repeatable workflow.
- **[[Tool Definition]]** -- Tool calls can read from or write to Deployment when automation needs structured side effects.

## Example Use Cases

- **System of record:** Teams create Deployment as the structured home for deployment record: environment, version, timestamp, status, rollback reference and keep related work linked instead of scattered across notes.
- **Automation handoff:** A [[Workflow Playbook]] or [[Trigger]] creates or updates Deployment when upstream conditions are met, reducing repetitive setup work.
- **Search and reporting:** Deployment stays queryable for dashboards, summaries, and downstream actions without re-entering the same information in multiple places.
